summary: Crisp audience tape captures an electrifying second set (Fire, Dark Star, epic Drums/Space) with strong organ work, while the first set is mixed.
review: The Atlanta recording delivers a powerful second half—huge Fire, jazzy Playin', a soaring Dark Star and one of the most intense modern‑day Drums/Space sequences—enhanced by Vince’s tasty organ textures. The audience source is clear and wide‑ranged, though volume levels are low and a few moments feel uneven; the first set receives mixed remarks, especially on HC Sunshine and Jack‑a‑Roe.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the second‑set performance and note good (if slightly low) audio clarity; weighted average of strong show quality (≈4.5) and solid recording quality (≈4.0) yields a ~4.3 rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear mic placement captures crowd ambience and wide frequency response; low overall volume and occasional uneven levels, but overall crisp and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is uneven with some weaker numbers, but the second set builds superbly, delivering deep jams and a memorable Drums/Space that drives the concert’s high point.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Starts half‑asleep on early songs, then becomes alert; strong guitar tone and vocal delivery on New Speedway Boogie, Stella Blue and Liberty.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince's organ work is highlighted as tasty and inventive, adding Dark Star motifs and enriching the jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s drumming in the Drums/Space is described as magical, intense, with ghostly choir samples and drones.
